summary: Reject inverted bounds (`--tilt-min > --tilt-max`, `--azimuth-min > --azimuth-max`) in `production sweep` and `production optimal-tilt` so the sentinel `e_y_kwh: -1` cannot leak into JSON output.
reason: Greptile review (P1) flagged that `best` was initialized with `EY: -1` (sweep) / `bestEY: -1.0` (optimal-tilt). With inverted bounds the loop body never executes, leaving the sentinel in place; the JSON output then carried `"e_y_kwh": -1` / `"best_e_y": -1` with `count: 0`. A machine consumer would have to cross-check `count` to know the value is bogus. Added an explicit pre-check that returns a clear `--tilt-min (X) must be <= --tilt-max (Y)` error before any API call.
validated_outcome: `production sweep --tilt-min 60 --tilt-max 0` now exits with `Error: --tilt-min (60) must be <= --tilt-max (0)`; same shape for `--azimuth-min > --azimuth-max` and for `production optimal-tilt`. Normal-case dry-runs still succeed.
